title: Diamond Crash Causes Health Crisis in Botswana
subtitle: As diamond sales drop, the country faces medicine shortages and economic uncertainty.

summary.en: Botswana is facing a difficult economic period due to a sharp decline in diamond prices. Diamonds make up the majority of the country's exports, and their falling value has hurt the national budget. This economic downturn has made it hard for the government to provide free healthcare. Many citizens are now struggling to afford essential medicines. Experts warn that long-standing issues with government agencies have made the situation even worse.
summary.zh: 波札那因鑽石價格急劇下跌，正面臨艱難的經濟時期。鑽石佔該國出口的大宗，其價值下滑重創了國家預算。這場經濟衰退導致政府難以提供免費醫療，許多公民現在難以負擔基本藥物。專家警告，政府機構長期存在的問題使情況雪上加霜。

paragraphs.en: For many years, Botswana was known as one of the most stable and wealthy countries in Africa. After gaining independence from Britain in 1966, the discovery of diamonds changed the nation's future. This wealth allowed the government to build a strong system for free education and become a global leader in fighting HIV/Aids. However, this success is now under pressure as the country faces a serious economic downturn.
paragraphs.zh: 多年來，波札那（Botswana）一直被視為非洲最穩定且富裕的國家之一。自一九六六年脫離英國獨立後，鑽石的發現徹底改變了該國的命運。這筆財富讓政府得以建立完善的免費教育體系，並成為全球對抗愛滋病（HIV/Aids）的領頭羊。然而，隨著該國面臨嚴重的經濟衰退，這份成就如今正承受巨大壓力。

paragraphs.en: The main cause of this trouble is a collapse in the global demand for natural diamonds. According to The Guardian, the price of these stones has fallen by 60% over the last four years. Many consumers are now choosing cheaper, lab-grown stones instead of natural ones. Because diamonds make up 80% of Botswana’s exports, this change has hit the national economy very hard. The International Monetary Fund (IMF) estimated that the country’s economy shrank by 3% in 2024 and by another 1% last year.
paragraphs.zh: 造成此困境的主因是全球天然鑽石需求崩盤。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，過去四年來，這類寶石的價格下跌了百分之六十。許多消費者現在轉而選擇價格較低的人造鑽石，而非天然鑽石。由於鑽石佔波札那出口總額的百分之八十，這項轉變對國家經濟造成了沉重打擊。國際貨幣基金組織（IMF）估計，該國經濟在二零二四年萎縮了百分之三，去年則進一步下滑了百分之一。

paragraphs.en: This economic decline has had a direct impact on the lives of ordinary people. In Botswana, public healthcare is supposed to be free for everyone. However, the government has struggled to keep up with the cost of medicine. Last year, President Duma Boko declared a public health emergency because of severe shortages of essential drugs and medical supplies. For citizens like Boitumelo Mosege, a 53-year-old former farmer, this has been life-changing. Mosege, who suffers from a thyroid condition, says she has often been unable to get the medicine she needs. She now relies on her family’s small income to pay for her own treatment, which she finds very difficult to afford.
paragraphs.zh: 經濟衰退直接影響了普通民眾的生活。在波札那，公共醫療理應對全民免費，但政府已難以負擔藥品成本。去年，總統杜馬·波科（Duma Boko）因基本藥物與醫療物資嚴重短缺，宣布進入公共衛生緊急狀態。對於像五十三歲的前農民波伊圖梅洛·莫塞格（Boitumelo Mosege）這樣的公民來說，這改變了她的人生。患有甲狀腺疾病的莫塞格表示，她經常無法取得所需的藥物。她現在只能依靠家人的微薄收入來支付治療費用，這讓她感到難以負荷。

paragraphs.en: Other families are facing similar struggles. Kelly Jansen, who cares for her elderly father, spends a large part of his monthly pension just to buy basic medical supplies and pay for his care. Jansen told The Guardian that she is looking for help to get an electric wheelchair for her father, noting that she wants her life back. These stories show how the lack of government support is forcing families to make difficult choices between food and health.
paragraphs.zh: 其他家庭也面臨類似的困境。負責照顧年邁父親的凱莉·詹森（Kelly Jansen），必須將父親每月領取的退休金大部分用於購買基本醫療用品與支付照護費用。詹森告訴《衛報》，她正在尋求協助，希望能為父親添購一台電動輪椅，並提到她希望能找回原本的生活。這些故事顯示，政府支援的匱乏正迫使家庭在食物與健康之間做出艱難的抉擇。

paragraphs.en: Experts suggest that the problem is not just about money, but also about how the government manages its resources. Thabo Lucas Seleke, a health policy lecturer at the University of Botswana, explained that the Central Medical Stores (CMS)—the agency responsible for buying medicine—has been failing for a long time. He stated that the agency is a place where corruption can easily happen and that it has not improved in many years. President Boko has also criticized the agency for raising drug prices during this difficult time.
paragraphs.zh: 專家指出，問題不僅在於資金，還在於政府如何管理資源。波札那大學（University of Botswana）的衛生政策講師塔博·盧卡斯·塞萊克（Thabo Lucas Seleke）解釋說，負責採購藥物的中央醫療倉庫（CMS）長期以來運作失靈。他表示，該機構是貪腐容易滋生的地方，且多年來毫無改善。波科總統也曾批評該機構在艱困時期調漲藥價。

paragraphs.en: While the government is trying to manage the crisis, the future remains uncertain. The country is also facing rising fuel prices, which will make things more expensive for everyone. Marisa Lourenço, an expert on the region, noted that because Botswana imports all of its oil, the high cost of fuel will be another major challenge for the economy. Despite these difficulties, the government has not provided a detailed plan on how it will fix the health procurement system or how it will recover from the loss of diamond income.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管政府正試圖應對危機，但未來仍充滿不確定性。該國同時面臨燃料價格上漲，這將導致所有人的生活成本增加。區域專家瑪麗莎·羅倫索（Marisa Lourenço）指出，由於波札那的所有石油皆仰賴進口，高昂的燃料成本將成為經濟的另一項重大挑戰。儘管困難重重，政府尚未針對如何修復醫療採購體系，或如何從鑽石收入損失中復甦，提出詳細計畫。

paragraphs.en: Botswana’s situation serves as a reminder of how much a country can depend on a single industry. For decades, diamonds provided the money needed to build a modern society. Now, as the world changes, the nation must find new ways to support its people. For citizens waiting for medicine, the need for a solution is urgent. As the country moves forward, the focus will likely remain on whether the government can reform its agencies and find a way to protect its most vulnerable people during this time of economic hardship.
paragraphs.zh: 波札那的處境提醒了世人，一個國家對單一產業的依賴程度有多深。數十年來，鑽石提供了建立現代社會所需的資金。如今，隨著世界局勢變遷，該國必須尋找新的方式來支持人民。對於等待藥物的公民而言，解決方案刻不容緩。隨著國家持續前行，未來的焦點將在於政府能否改革相關機構，並在經濟困頓時期找到保護最弱勢族群的方法。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have a strong negative effect on someone or something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 對……造成嚴重打擊或負面影響。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「打擊」的物理動作，這裡指經濟上的嚴重衝擊。文中：Because diamonds make up 80% of Botswana’s exports, this change has hit the national economy very hard.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be able to afford or manage the speed or cost of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指政府無法負擔持續增加的醫療成本。文中：However, the government has struggled to keep up with the cost of medicine.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To constitute or form a part of a whole.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「化妝」或「編造」，這裡指佔據總體比例。文中：Because diamonds make up 80% of Botswana’s exports, this change has hit the national economy very hard.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: ‘I want my life back’: drugs shortages lay bare economic impact of diamond crash in Botswana

quiz.question: What is the primary reason for Botswana's current economic downturn according to the text?

options.A: A decrease in the government's ability to manage public healthcare costs.
options.B: A significant drop in global demand for natural diamonds.
options.C: The rising cost of fuel and oil imports for the country.
options.D: Corruption within the Central Medical Stores agency.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，文中明確指出經濟困境的主因是全球對天然鑽石的需求崩潰（collapse in the global demand for natural diamonds）。選項 A 是經濟衰退導致的結果而非主因；選項 C 是另一個挑戰，但非導致經濟衰退的核心原因；選項 D 是關於醫療採購的問題，屬於政府治理層面，而非國家經濟衰退的根源。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the impact of the diamond industry on Botswana's social progress?

options.A: The diamond industry has always been a source of corruption for the government.
options.B: Botswana's social successes were largely funded by its diamond wealth.
options.C: The government never intended to use diamond money for public services.
options.D: The transition to lab-grown diamonds was planned by the government to improve the economy.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段與最後一段，鑽石發現後改變了國家的未來，並讓政府有資金建立免費教育與對抗 HIV/Aids 的系統。這暗示了過去的社會進步高度依賴鑽石收入。選項 A 無法由文中推論出「一直以來」都是腐敗來源；選項 C 與文章事實矛盾；選項 D 文中並未提及這是政府的計畫，反而是市場趨勢導致的結果。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'this change has hit the national economy very hard,' what does 'hit' most closely mean?

options.B: Reached a specific level or target.
options.C: Had a negative effect on something.
options.D: Suddenly increased in size or intensity.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。在文中，hit 用於描述經濟受到鑽石需求下降的負面衝擊。選項 A 是字面上的物理動作，在此語境下不適用；選項 B 通常用於 hit a target 或 hit a record，不符合此處語法；選項 D 雖然有影響力，但 hit 在此處強調的是「打擊」或「造成損害」，而非指經濟規模的增加。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: Botswana must immediately stop relying on diamond exports to survive.
options.B: The government is successfully reforming its healthcare system despite the economic crisis.
options.C: Botswana faces a difficult future as its reliance on a single industry creates severe social and economic challenges.
options.D: The rise of lab-grown diamonds will eventually lead to a more stable economy for Botswana.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文整體論述了波札那因過度依賴鑽石產業，在鑽石需求下降後，導致國家經濟衰退並進一步影響民眾的醫療權益，強調了單一產業結構帶來的脆弱性。選項 A 雖然是建議，但非文章總結；選項 B 與事實不符，政府尚未提出詳細改革計畫；選項 D 與文意相反，實驗室鑽石是造成目前經濟危機的原因之一。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
